title: connector CLI builds entrypoints via Vite into static artifact
description: Wire Vite's build API into the connector CLI to build all synthetic entrypoints (from TASK-40) into a single static artifact directory (HTML/JS/CSS) ready for upload.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI command `systembook-connector build [--root <dir>] [--out <dir>]` runs discovery, generation, and a Vite multi-entry build in sequence
Vite build uses `build.rollupOptions.input` populated with all generated entry `index.html` files, output to `--out` (default `.systembook/dist`)
Build output is a self-contained static directory: opening any variant's built `index.html` directly renders that component variant with no missing asset references
Build fails loudly (non-zero exit code, clear error message) if any entry fails to compile, rather than silently producing a partial artifact
Running the full `build` command against the TASK-39 fixture repo produces a working static artifact for each valid variant

steps.description: Add Vite as a build dependency
Chain discover → generate → build in the CLI
steps.details: In `packages/connector/src/cli.ts`, implement `build` command: call the TASK-39 discovery function, then TASK-40's generation function, collecting all generated `index.html` paths.

steps.description: Configure and invoke Vite's build API programmatically
steps.details: Use Vite's JS API (`build()` from `'vite'`) with `build: { rollupOptions: { input: { ...entryNameToHtmlPath } }, outDir: options.out } `, `plugins: [react()]`.
Catch and rethrow Vite build errors with `process.exitCode = 1` and a message identifying which entry failed, rather than letting the process exit 0 on partial failure.

steps.description: Verify a built artifact renders standalone
steps.details: Run the full build against the fixture repo, then serve the output directory with a static file server and open a variant's HTML in a browser (or headless via Playwright) to confirm it renders the component with no console errors about missing assets.

technicalNotes: This build step runs inside the *consuming team's* CI (per the PRD's architecture), not inside the SystemBook instance itself — keep the connector's dependencies self-contained so it can run in an arbitrary repo's CI environment with just `npx` or as an installed devDependency.

Relative asset paths matter: since each variant's artifact may be served from a nested path on the SystemBook instance later (TASK-46), verify the Vite build's `base` config doesn't hardcode an absolute path that breaks when re-hosted.
